user_message: [AUTOMATION TRIGGER] Scheduled Calendar event with non-Microsoft external attendee detected initiated for Wide World Importers.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-02 11:09 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Wide World Importers consumption trend

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about consumption trend. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
Date: 2024-02-16 15:49 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Wide World Importers consumption trend

Just checking in on the consumption trend proposal. Our Director of IT is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Ryan Kowalski <ryan.kowalski@wideworldimp.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-04 10:37 EST
Subject: URGENT: consumption trend Issue at Wide World Importers

Our team encountered some challenges with consumption trend. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

Meeting: consumption trend Discussion - Wide World Importers
Date: 2024-01-15
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft)

[00:01:15] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is consumption trend.

[00:05:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): The timeline for budget approval is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:10:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about partner engagement. Can we address those?

[00:14:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is integration complexity.

[00:18:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about budget approval. Can we address those?

[00:20:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is performance requirements.

Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct consumption trend decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence
